vision: It's a briefing tool for busy professionals. I want to help executives and researchers who don't have time to read 50 articles a day — they get a single AI-powered summary every morning. The tech is Next.js on Vercel, and I care most about speed, reliability, and a great UX. I don't want to build social features or real-time collaboration — just clean, fast briefings.

clarifications.question: Who's the primary audience — executives scanning headlines over coffee, or researchers doing deep technical dives?
clarifications.answer: Mostly executives and team leads. They check it on their phone during their commute — so it has to be fast and scannable. No long-form deep dives.

clarifications.question: You mentioned no social features or real-time collab. Anything else you want to explicitly rule out so I keep the scope tight?
clarifications.answer: No content creation tools, no multi-user dashboards. It's single-player — you get your briefing, you read it, done.

audience: Executives and team leads who consume briefings on mobile

pushback.comment: Almost — add multi-language support to non-goals, English only for now. And say 'time-poor professionals' instead of just executives, it's broader than that.

revised.audience: Time-poor professionals who consume briefings on mobile

ticketOverrides.S1-1: Set up delivery cron with <5min end-to-end target
ticketOverrides.S1-2: Build processing pipeline with async queue
ticketOverrides.S2-1: Add briefing status tracking in Supabase
ticketOverrides.S3-1: Build subscriber signup with welcome email
